analysis.summary: The technician performed network checks using iperf3 and ping from a Windows machine to a Linux server. The primary focus of the session was an unsuccessful attempt to configure and log into an iSCSI target from the Linux server, with the technician repeatedly trying variations of the `iscsiadm` command without achieving a successful login.
actions_taken: Attempted iSCSI target login on a Linux server (TE-DC-MD-NC-02) using `iscsiadm` commands, which repeatedly failed.
Performed iSCSI target discovery using `iscsiadm --mode discovery` on the Linux server (TE-DC-MD-NC-02).
Checked disk utilization (`df -h`) on the Linux server (TE-DC-MD-NC-02).
Pinged an IP address (10.7.9.250) from the Linux server (TE-DC-MD-NC-02).
Ran a network performance test server (`iperf3 -s`) on the Windows client machine.
Reread partition table (`partprobe`) on the Linux server (TE-DC-MD-NC-02).
Checked disk block devices (`lsblk`) on the Linux server (TE-DC-MD-NC-02).
Confirmed display settings on the Windows client machine.
issues_observed: Repeated 'No records found' error when attempting iSCSI login using `iscsiadm`.
'Error while adding record!' message during an `iscsiadm` operation.
A typo `lblk` resulted in 'command not found'.
The final `iscsiadm --login` command did not produce output within the recorded timeframe, potentially indicating a hang or prolonged operation.
notable_events: The technician spent a considerable amount of time (approx. 32 seconds of active attempts) repeatedly trying different iSCSI login commands with varying target names and identifiers without success.
The session ended with an `iscsiadm` command seemingly hanging or not producing output for an extended period (approx. 24 seconds).
concerns: The core task of iSCSI login remained unresolved at the end of the session, indicating a lack of successful progress.
The repeated, unsuccessful attempts at iSCSI login suggest a possible lack of systematic troubleshooting or in-depth knowledge of iSCSI configuration.

stuck_details: From 00:16-00:48, the technician repeatedly attempted `iscsiadm --mode node --targetname ... --login` using different variations of target names and the full IQN, receiving 'No records found' or 'Error while adding record!'. This pattern indicates they were stuck on how to successfully log into the iSCSI target for approximately 32 seconds of active command input/waiting.

From 01:29-01:53, after entering the final `iscsiadm --mode node --targetname iqn.2004-04.com.qnap.ts-ec880u:iscsi.vaf-md-nc.2cb40e --login` command, there was no visible output for the remainder of the recording (approximately 24 seconds), suggesting the command might have hung or the technician was stuck waiting for a result.
tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician seemed uncertain and employed a trial-and-error approach during the iSCSI configuration, specifically when repeatedly attempting to log into the iSCSI target. Commands were re-entered with minor variations, or the same discovery command was run again, without a clear path to resolution.
coaching_flags: Review best practices for iSCSI target login and `iscsiadm` command usage, focusing on understanding target discovery output and correct login syntax.
Encourage a more systematic troubleshooting approach when commands repeatedly fail, rather than re-typing slight variations.
Discuss strategies for diagnosing and handling commands that appear to hang or do not produce immediate output.
